name: Toasted Pecan Soup
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup chopped fennel bulb or celery
recipeInstructions:
1. Spread the pecans in an ovenproof pan and toast in the oven, stirring frequently, until lightly browned and fragrant, about 15 minutes. Chop coarsely and set aside.
2. In a soup pot, melt the butter over low heat. Add the shallot, leek, and fennel or celery and cook, stirring frequently, until tender, about 10 minutes.
3. Add 2 cups of the toasted pecans (reserve the rest for garnish), brown sugar, cinnamon, thyme, and bay leaf and cook, stirring frequently, for about 5 minutes.
4. Stir in the broth and bring almost to a boil over medium heat. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Reduce the heat to low and simmer, uncovered, until the pecans are very tender, about 2 hours. Discard the bay leaf.
5. Working in batches, if necessary, transfer the soup to a food processor or blender and puree until smooth.
6. Pour the soup into a clean pot and bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce the heat to low, stir in the cream, Worcestershire sauce, and bourbon or sherry and simmer until the alcohol is evaporated about 2 minutes.
7. To serve hot, ladle into prehealed soup bowls and garnish with the reserved toasted pecans. Alternatively, pour into a container and refrigerate, uncovered, until cool, then tightly cover and store for up to 3 days. Slowly reheat before garnishing and serving; do not boil.
8. To serve cold, pour into a container and refrigerate, uncovered, until cool, then tightly cover and chill for at least 2 hours or as long as 3 days. Remove from the refrigerator about 25 minutes before serving. Ladle into chilled bowls and garnish.
